text: My LES go-to. The shio broth is clean and bright, and they swapped the spicy red chili ramen to skip the dairy butter without blinking — lactose-friendly and still rich. Light through the front window hits the bowl beautifully around 4pm. Shot here for Tangerine last fall.

Northern Thai done with real heat. Naturally dairy-free across most of the menu — the jungle curry and kao soi (ask for no cream) are stunning on the plate, deep ochre tones. Tight little room, warm tungsten light. Carroll Gardens gem.
Adobada off the trompo, corn tortillas, no cheese needed — easy dairy-free order. Fast, cheap, photogenic char on the meat. Bedford location is my quick lunch when I'm tethered all morning.

Chef Ana's place, and I'm biased — I shoot the seasonal menus here. Portuguese-Brazilian, generous earth tones, and she always flags the dairy for me (the coconut moqueca is safe and gorgeous). South light off N 6th in the late afternoon is a dream for plating.

Morning oat-milk latte stop before yoga. They stock real oat milk (not the watery stuff) so it's an easy dairy-free order, and the foam photographs clean. Kent Ave windows let in soft north light. A touch pricey but consistent.

Fully plant-based, so the whole menu is dairy-free by default — huge relief. The 'mac & shews' looks the part for a photo and actually tastes good. Lighting inside is moody, needed my own fill, but textures are great.

Backyard garden is the move — dappled natural light all afternoon. Italian, so I steer around the cheese, but the kitchen happily did the cacio e pepe-style pasta with olive oil instead. Rustic plating, warm tones. Grand St staple.

Oysters and absinthe, dim and gorgeous. Most of the raw bar is naturally dairy-free; I skip the buttered items. Hard to photograph in that low light without a tripod, but the bar itself is a set piece. Bedford Ave classic.

Where my family does dim sum — Flushing roots, but this LES branch holds up. Har gow, turnip cake, no dairy in sight, easy for me. Bright fluorescent room is tough light, but the carts make for fun candid frames.

Moroccan brunch in Williamsburg. The tagines and the basteeya can be done dairy-free if you ask; staff is good about it. Tiled tabletops and warm light make every plate read like a still life. Berry St favorite.

Tiny ichiju-sansai counter, all Japanese precision. Naturally dairy-free menu and the most photogenic set meal in the neighborhood — grilled fish, pickles, miso, all earth tones on raw ceramic. Soft daylight from the side. Books up fast.

Greenpoint bakery with serious styling already built in — vintage tile, linen, morning light pouring in. Most of the loaves and the jam thumbprints are dairy-free; I confirm before I bite. Came twice this week, shot the croissant case both times.
Dairy-free menu is real and honored. Chef Ana was gracious.
Window light at 4 pm is unreal. Booked the corner table.
Shot the pork bao here for the Tangerine feature — kitchen let me tether at the pass.
Order the black cod. It plates like a painting on the slate.
Coffee is fine, but the surface reflectivity killed my flat-lay attempt.
Family-friendly at 5 pm, camera-friendly at 3 pm. Mom loved it.
Family-friendly at 5 pm, camera-friendly at 3 pm. Mom loved it.
Vegan menu section makes catering calls easy.
Broth photographs beautifully because of the fat cap. Front window is best.
Booked as tasting for the Estrela cookbook chapter — dairy alternates offered without asking.
Handrolls at 6 pm, natural light window. The counter is warm-toned wood.
